title: AI Trust & Safety Digest
tagline: A curated current snapshot of AI trust, safety, and governance, including as many materially important fresh items as the day warrants.
lead: The clearest signal today is operationalization: AI safety work is moving from abstract principles into product behavior, enterprise risk processes, and incident response. The strongest updates focus on context-aware safeguards in sensitive conversations, practical risk-management guidance, and software supply-chain security around AI systems.

items.title: OpenAI updates ChatGPT to better recognize context in sensitive conversations
items.why_it_matters: Improved context tracking can reduce unsafe responses in high-risk, evolving user interactions.

items.title: Partnership on AI outlines how to move from theory to action in AI risk management
items.why_it_matters: It pushes organizations toward concrete governance and risk-management practices rather than high-level principles.

items.title: OpenAI details its response to the TanStack npm supply chain attack
items.why_it_matters: Supply-chain compromises directly affect AI system integrity, deployment security, and user trust.

note: Selected from recent trust-and-safety relevant posts and papers by labs, evaluators, standards bodies, policy organizations, and arXiv, then summarized with GPT-5.4. Includes as many materially important fresh items as the day warrants.
